what_it_does: Implements a deterministic, model-free SEED lifter named `links` that reads a markdown unit and returns a deduplicated, order-preserving list of outbound link targets found in `[[wikilinks]]` and `[markdown](links)`.
observations: Uses two regexes: one for `[[wikilinks]]` capturing the target before any `#` or `|` fragment/alias, and one for markdown links with a negative lookbehind `(?<!!)` to skip image syntax.
Returns `[]` for non-string input or empty text, treating absence as structural rather than error.
Defines a `LIFTER` registry dict with `id`, `extract`, and `desc`, and the docstring states `id` must equal the file stem `links`.
Docstring references `runtime/lifter_registry.py`, `lifters/AGENTS.md`, and a `produced_by:"code"` projection framing.
No external dependencies or model calls; pure regex extraction.
standards: The markdown-link regex explicitly excludes image links via `(?<!!)`.
Deduplication is order-preserving by linear `seen` list rather than set, preserving first-encounter order.
It is framed as a deterministic 'floor' reader: a parse is a read, not an interpretation.
conventions: Uses `from __future__ import annotations` and type hints.
Follows the lifter registry contract: exposes `LIFTER` with `id`, `extract`, `desc`.
Keeps the lifter `id` equal to the file stem.
Docstrings describe behavior, edge cases, and registry integration.
concerns: Wikilink regex drops anchors and display aliases by only capturing up to the first `#` or `|`.
Markdown link regex may mis-handle nested parentheses or brackets inside link text/URL.
Reference-style markdown links (`[text][ref]`) are not extracted.
No URL validation; any non-empty captured group is kept.
notes: A small, deterministic markdown link extractor meant to be registered as a code-projection lifter with no model involvement.
questions: How does `lifter_registry.py` invoke `_extract` — are additional arguments ever passed beyond `text` and `meta`?
Should wikilink targets be normalized for case or whitespace before deduplication?
Are bare URLs or autolinks (`<http://...>`) intentionally out of scope?
How is the `produced_by:"code"` projection consumed downstream?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to deterministically extract outbound links from markdown, and the implementation does exactly that for wikilinks and inline markdown links.
intention_signals: It appears designed to provide a fast, model-free floor-level reader that surfaces outbound references from markdown units so downstream layers can build a link graph or context map.
novelty: The explicit 'floor holds' framing — that a parse is a read, not an interpretive act — and the `produced_by:"code"` projection concept are unusual for a simple link extractor.
contribution: A deterministic, dependency-free function that extracts and deduplicates outbound wikilink and markdown-link targets from text, packaged as a reusable lifter for a registry-based extraction system.
summary_for_embedding: `lifters/links.py` defines a deterministic SEED lifter that extracts outbound `[[wikilink]]` and `[markdown](link)` targets from a markdown unit and returns them as a deduplicated, order-preserving list.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a model-free, registry-compatible code projection that reads structural outbound links from markdown content.

symbols.does: Deterministically extracts outbound wikilink and markdown-link targets from text, returning a deduplicated, order-preserving list.
symbols.description: `_extract` is a pure function that scans the input string with two regular expressions: one for `[[wikilink]]` targets (stopping at any `#` or `|` fragment/alias) and one for inline markdown links (excluding image syntax via a negative lookbehind). It filters empty captures, preserves first-encounter order through a linear `seen` list, and returns `[]` for non-string or empty input.
